summary_strategy: Position Conner as a builder-focused Application Security Engineer who ships production-grade security systems by embedding controls into CI/CD, automating remediation at scale, and translating compliance requirements into operational runbooks — matching EasyPost’s pragmatic, deployable-controls tone.

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, Rust, Go (explicitly called out in JD), then keep all 11 languages — drop C/C++ from top placement but retain them at end since JD emphasizes Python/Rust/Go proficiency
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to highlight vulnerability remediation automation, endpoint management (EDR/XDR), SOAR, and patch orchestration first; add CrowdStrike Falcon/RTR and Defender XDR from Skills Inventory as they’re directly relevant to endpoint hardening workflows
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Rename section title to 'AI for SecOps' only if allowed — but per instructions, must keep EXACT name 'AI/ML and Research'; reorder to lead with RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases, NLP for security context, and retrieval tuning — align with JD’s 'Intelligent Notifications' and 'AI governance' requirements
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to prioritize Flask, FastAPI (for API-driven security tooling), then Docker, React.js — highlight internal tooling use cases matching JD’s 'Product Innovation' and 'Web and Internal Security Tooling'
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to lead with CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, release automation, and container orchestration — explicitly include GitHub Actions/GitLab CI from Skills Inventory to match 'DevSecOps Adoption' requirement
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder PostgreSQL, MySQL, DynamoDB first (common in cloud-native apps), then vector databases and Snowflake — keep all 10 as required; emphasize data pipelines for security analytics where relevant

experience_focus.company: University of California, Office of the President
experience_focus.must_highlight: deployable security controls at scale, API-driven vulnerability response automation, AI/ML-powered SecOps tooling with measurable operational impact
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no vague claims like 'transformed security posture' — stick to concrete actions: built, architected, engineered, implemented

experience_focus.must_highlight: cross-platform endpoint automation (macOS/Windows), PowerShell/Python automation at scale, phishing analytics with compliance outcomes
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 200 hours saved annually, 500 hours reduced workload, 25 percent compliance improvement
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no speculative ownership like 'owned security outcomes' — use 'developed', 'built', 'engineered'

experience_focus.must_highlight: CI/CD-integrated security gates, automated accessibility testing (WCAG), cross-browser validation — tie directly to 'DevSecOps Adoption' and 'Product Innovation'
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no inflated scope like 'enterprise-wide tooling' — keep freelance context accurate

risk_controls: Never invent metrics beyond those explicitly listed in experience (e.g., no 'improved MTTR by X%' unless stated)
Never use em dashes, en dashes, or ellipses — replace with commas or parentheses
Avoid phrases like 'leveraged', 'synergized', 'holistic' — use active verbs: built, engineered, implemented, architected
Do not add responsibilities or tools not present in baseline resume or Skills Inventory mapping — e.g., do not invent Terraform/Ansible unless implied by 'Infrastructure as Code'
Do not rename sections or categories — keep 'AI/ML and Research' verbatim
